dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9154 IH: Shrimp Honesty and Responsibility in Import Monitoring Protocols Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Mace (for herself, Mr. Weber of Texas, Mr. Carter of Louisiana, and Mr. Ezell) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Natural Resources, and in addition to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned

form.official-title: To direct the Secretary of Commerce to develop a methodology for identifying country of origin of shrimp, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Shrimp Honesty and Responsibility in Import Monitoring Protocols Act of 2026 or the SHRIMP Act of 2026.

section.header: Methodology for identifying country of origin of shrimp

subsection.text: Not later than 18 months after the date of enactment of this Act, the Secretary, in consultation with the Commissioner of U.S. Customs and Border Protection, and the Commandant of the Coast Guard, shall develop a methodology for identifying country of origin of shrimp to—

paragraph.text: support Federal and State law enforcement agencies in combating illegal, unreported, and unregulated fishing;

subparagraph.text: the traceability of shrimp in the food supply; and
screening of shrimp at ports of entry;

subsection.text: The methodology developed under subsection (a) shall—

paragraph.text: include the use of chemical analysis;
be responsive to the needs of Federal and State law enforcement agencies in combating illegal, unreported, and unregulated fishing;
minimize the time necessary to obtain results of tests conducted using such methodology;
be capable of being made available in the form of a field kit that can be easily carried by 1 individual; and
be usable for the testing of—

subsection.text: Not later than 2 years after the date of enactment of this Act, the Secretary shall submit to the Committees on Transportation and Infrastructure and Natural Resources of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate a report that includes the following:

paragraph.text: A summary of the methodology developed under subsection (a).
A plan for operationalizing the methodology developed under subsection (a).
For any aspect of the methodology developed under subsection (a) that the Secretary determines cannot be easily operationalized, an explanation of—

subparagraph.text: why such aspect cannot be easily operationalized;
whether additional research would yield an alternative to such aspect that could be easily operationalized; and

if such aspect is related to the use of chemical analysis required under subsection (b)(1), whether a methodology based on a technique other than chemical analysis could be more easily operationalized.

paragraph.text: The term Secretary means the Secretary of Commerce, acting through—

subparagraph.text: the Under Secretary of Commerce for Oceans and Atmosphere in the Under Secretary’s capacity as Administrator of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration; and
the Under Secretary of Commerce for Standards and Technology in the Under Secretary’s capacity as Director of the National Institute of Standards and Technology.

paragraph.text: The term shrimp means any member of a species of the suborder Dendrobranchiata or the infraorder Caridea that is—

subparagraph.text: harvested in the United States for human consumption;
cultivated in the United States for human consumption; or
imported to the United States for human consumption.
